feedback.text: Dear Sir/Madam, I'd like to sugget a potential addition to the Digital Fairness Act. Software is frequently made unusable by authors after purchase, without any option to continue using the last published/purchased version after support has ended. These acts by software authors effectively restrict/remove access to software that was purchased after the purchase was made. This is especially prevalent in, but not exclusive to, the gaming industry. See also the Stop Killing Games initiative. I think that it is important to consider appending rules to prevent these aforementioned practices by software authors to the Digital Fairness Act. Thank you.
